## API contracts (not corpus-derived)

These statements describe API-level invariants the file format depends on. The extractor cannot prove them — it only counts corpus violations. Zero observed violations is not a proof.

### tool_use_tool_result_pairing

Every tool_use block in an assistant message MUST be followed by a matching tool_result block (tool_result.tool_use_id === tool_use.id) in a subsequent user turn within the same session. The Anthropic API rejects sessions with orphaned tool_use or tool_result blocks.

**Enforced at:** `src/main/sessions/claude/adapter.ts:57`, `src/main/sessions/claude/adapter.ts:697`

**Observed violations:** 304 — see samples below

Violation samples:

- `orphan tool_result tool_use_id=toolu_01KyC5xLZpQSR6MhBuHk9TdX at 1af5ca21-177e-465e-9be8-4cf64038daad.jsonl`
- `orphan tool_result tool_use_id=toolu_01VctGAJKCMXtKgfGT1j49p2 at 1af5ca21-177e-465e-9be8-4cf64038daad.jsonl`
- `unmatched tool_use id=toolu_01KyC5xLZpQSR6MhBuHk9TdX at 1af5ca21-177e-465e-9be8-4cf64038daad.jsonl`

### parent_uuid_chain

Claude Code appends lines in parent->child order; each line's parentUuid should reference a uuid earlier in the same file. Breaking the chain can cause Claude Code to misconstruct the conversation view on resume.

**Enforced at:** `src/main/sessions/claude/adapter.ts:697`

**Observed violations:** 0 (no corpus record disproved this contract — not a proof of correctness)

### visible_type_preservation

The adapter treats only user/assistant/system (with isSidechain !== true) as visible, editable messages. All other types (summary, custom-title, attachment, file-history-snapshot, last-prompt, pr-link) must be preserved verbatim across edits — Claude Code's resume flow, cache, and UI all depend on them.

**Enforced at:** `src/main/sessions/claude/adapter.ts:57`, `src/main/sessions/claude/adapter.ts:60`

**Observed violations:** 0 (no corpus record disproved this contract — not a proof of correctness)
